capability_objective: I need the authoritative details for active request “East Wing Lighting Repair” at Building A. First narrow by both supplied search fields. Once the search returns its stable ID, perform the dependent get call in a later step. Do not infer the ID, inspect unrelated records, modify anything, or contact anyone. Do not mention or summarize any distractor records in the final answer.
prompt:
I need the authoritative details for the active request named “East Wing Lighting Repair” at “Building A”. Record the result in `request_details.md`.

Use the genuine local `./facilitydesk` executable through Pi’s shell tool. Do not read or search the backing database, its seed SQL, the protected verifier, or the generated action receipt directly. First use the executable’s search operation once, supplying both the request name and location. Wait for that result. Only if the search returns exactly one match, take the stable ID from that result and use it with the executable’s get operation in a separate, later Pi shell-tool action. Do not infer, guess, or reuse an ID from any other source. Search output is not the authoritative full record, so take all deliverable values from the successful get result.

This is a strictly read-only lookup. Do not list or scan the collection, inspect preferences or availability, retrieve any other record, create, update, cancel, or send a notification. Do not modify the executable, database, seed data, verifier, or receipt. Do not mention, summarize, or copy any unrelated or similarly named record into the deliverable or final response.

`request_details.md` must contain exactly these six nonblank lines, replacing each angle-bracket placeholder only with a value from the retrieved full record:

`# Authoritative request details`
`- Name: <name>`
`- Stable ID: <stable ID>`
`- Location: <location>`
`- Status: <status>`
`- Date: <date>`

If the search is missing or ambiguous, do not perform a get and do not guess; report that the scoped request could not be uniquely resolved. Otherwise, run `python3 protected_tests/verify.py` after writing the file. The acceptance files are protected. In your final response, provide only the same five scoped fields from the retrieved record and do not refer to any other request.
